name: Chiang Mai
tagline: The Original Digital Nomad Capital — Retire for Less in Thailand's Rose of the North
description: Chiang Mai is the undisputed champion of affordable FIRE living in Southeast Asia. With a thriving expat community, world-class temples, lush mountain scenery, and a cost of living that lets you stretch every dollar, it remains the gold standard for early retirees seeking quality of life on a modest portfolio.

quickFacts.visa: 60-day tourist visa on arrival; Long-Term Resident (LTR) visa available for retirees with $80K+ income or $1M+ assets; Thai Elite visa ($15K–$30K) for 5–20 year stays
quickFacts.safetyRating: Very Safe — low violent crime, occasional petty theft in tourist areas
quickFacts.nearestAirport: Chiang Mai International Airport (CNX) — direct flights to Bangkok, Singapore, Kuala Lumpur
quickFacts.climate: Tropical savanna with three seasons: hot (Mar–May), rainy (Jun–Oct), cool (Nov–Feb)

housing.description: Modern 1-bedroom condo in Nimman or Old City area with pool and gym access

dining.description: Street food and local Thai restaurants daily; occasional Western café meals

healthcare.description: Basic private insurance covering outpatient visits at quality hospitals like Ram Hospital

transportation.description: Rented scooter or songthaew rides around the city

entertainment.description: Coworking space membership, temple visits, weekend markets, and local hiking

1M.lifestyle: A clean, modern 1-bedroom in the Nimman area with pool access. Mostly cooking at home with cheap street food a few times a week. Getting around by songthaew and motorbike. Basic private hospital coverage. Comfortable and affordable, though options for Western groceries and nightlife are limited.

housing.description: Spacious 2-bedroom condo or small townhouse in a desirable neighborhood with modern amenities

dining.description: Mix of Thai street food, upscale local restaurants, and regular Western dining

healthcare.description: Comprehensive private health insurance with dental; access to top-tier hospitals

transportation.description: Own scooter plus occasional Grab car rides and weekend road trips

entertainment.description: Gym membership, regular Thai cooking classes, weekend trips to Pai or Chiang Rai, streaming services

2M.lifestyle: A well-furnished 2-bedroom condo or townhouse with room for a home office. Dining out several times a week at local and Western restaurants without watching the bill. Own scooter plus Grab for convenience. Good private health insurance and dental. Weekend trips to Pai or Chiang Rai are easy and cheap. Day-to-day life is comfortable and unstressed.

housing.description: Luxury 3-bedroom pool villa in the outskirts or premium high-rise condo with city views

dining.description: Fine dining at top restaurants several times a week; regular sushi, Italian, and Thai tasting menus

healthcare.description: Premium international health insurance with full coverage including medical evacuations

transportation.description: Leased car or SUV, Grab for nights out, occasional domestic flights

entertainment.description: Premium gym, golf memberships, spa days, cultural festivals, weekend trips across northern Thailand

3M.lifestyle: A private pool villa on the outskirts or a premium high-rise condo with city views. Dining at the best restaurants in Chiang Mai regularly, including good sushi, Italian, and Thai tasting menus. A leased car or SUV for weekend drives through northern Thailand. Premium international health insurance. At this budget in Chiang Mai, very little is out of reach.

housing.description: Stunning private estate or luxury villa compound with pool, gardens, and mountain views

dining.description: Private chef for most meals plus frequent fine dining at Chiang Mai's top establishments

healthcare.description: Top-tier international insurance with Bumrungrad Bangkok access and annual executive health checks

transportation.description: Owned or leased luxury vehicle, private driver available, regular domestic flights

entertainment.description: Country club memberships, private yoga instruction, art collecting, regular travel across Southeast Asia

domesticHelp.description: Full-time housekeeper/cook, part-time gardener, and regular laundry service

luxuryExtras.description: Monthly spa packages, bespoke tailoring, premium wine subscriptions, charitable donations to local temples

5M.lifestyle: A large villa with pool and gardens in a desirable area. A housekeeper several times a week and a cook for most meals. A nice car with domestic flights to Bangkok or the islands whenever you want. Premium international health insurance with annual executive checkups. This budget goes very far in Chiang Mai — daily life is genuinely affluent with significant room to spare.

housing.description: Sprawling luxury compound with multiple buildings, infinity pool, and panoramic Doi Suthep views; possibly a second condo in Bangkok

dining.description: Personal chef team, imported ingredients flown in, plus unlimited fine dining and private dinner parties

healthcare.description: Concierge-level global health coverage; annual checkups at Bumrungrad; access to specialists anywhere in Asia

transportation.description: Luxury vehicle collection, full-time personal driver, frequent first-class domestic flights and regional travel

entertainment.description: Exclusive memberships, private art studio, philanthropic projects, regular international travel across Asia and beyond

domesticHelp.description: Full household staff including chef, housekeeper, gardener, driver, and property manager

luxuryExtras.description: Bespoke wellness programs, art commissions, luxury shopping trips to Bangkok, charitable foundations, investment in local businesses

10M.lifestyle: A large, well-appointed property with mountain views and possibly a second condo in Bangkok. A housekeeper, a cook a few times a week, a gardener, and a driver. First-class flights within Asia and business class for longer trips. Concierge-level health coverage. This is far more budget than Chiang Mai can absorb — daily life is very comfortable with no meaningful financial constraints, though the city itself remains a small, quiet place with limited high-end infrastructure.
